color.$description: Semantic roles for the neo-brutalist visual language over the Doctor School brand. Light-first; the .dark overrides live in semantic.dark.json. Brand primary = blue.500 #2D84F2 stays the brand ANCHOR (link accents, icons, tints, focus). White-on-fill AA (ADR-0013 §7): white on blue.500 is only 3.69:1, so a NORMAL-weight filled action does NOT paint blue.500 — it uses the accessible action-fill triad `primary-action` (blue.700, white 8.14:1, resting), `primary-hover`/`primary-pressed` (blue.800, 11.12:1), which keeps a visible resting→hover delta while clearing AA on every state. The same blue.700 rule governs any text-bearing brand FILL: `primary-surface` (blue.700) is the brand fill for a panel carrying normal-weight white copy. Text tiers: `foreground` ink / `muted-foreground` / `faint` / `muted-2` (neutral 900/600/500/400). Frame: structural `border` is the hard near-black outline (neutral.900), `hairline` (neutral.200) the subtle divider. Status = success green, warning amber (dark ink — white fails AA on amber), danger `destructive` red. `header` is the brand chrome band. `tint` is the pale-blue info surface. `elevation`/`elevation-soft` are the theme-aware cast-shadow tones the offset shadows reference.

background.$description: The default page/app surface (neo-brutalist page = flat white). Pair with `foreground` for text.

foreground.$description: Ink — the default text and icon colour, used on `background`.

section.$description: A faintly-tinted block surface used to band a section apart from the flat page, without a card border.

card.$description: A raised surface (card, popover, panel) sitting on `background`, framed by the hard `border`. Pair with `card-foreground`.

card-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `card` surface.

primary.$description: Brand anchor (blue.500) for link accents, focus, icons and tints. NOT a normal-weight filled action — that fails AA; use `primary-action`.

primary-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour ON a primary/action fill (white on the brand button).

primary-action.$description: Resting fill for a normal-weight filled primary button (blue.700 = #114D9E, white 8.14:1). Pairs with `primary-foreground`. Also the AA link-text colour.

primary-hover.$description: Hover fill for a primary action (darker than the resting `primary-action`).

primary-pressed.$description: Pressed/active fill for a primary action.

primary-surface.$description: Brand-coloured panel/surface that carries normal-weight white copy (e.g. the auth brand panel).

secondary.$description: Low-emphasis tonal fill (secondary buttons, subtle chips). Pair with `secondary-foreground`.

secondary-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `secondary` fill.

muted.$description: A muted background for subtle fills, hovered rows or disabled tracks.

muted-foreground.$description: De-emphasised text — helper text, placeholders, captions, metadata (neutral.600).

faint.$description: Third text tier below `muted-foreground` — timestamps, tertiary metadata (neutral.500).

muted-2.$description: Faintest text tier — disabled labels, decorative captions (neutral.400). Non-body use only; below the AA body-text threshold by design.

accent.$description: Hover/selected background for interactive list items and ghost buttons. Pair with `accent-foreground`.

accent-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on an `accent` surface.

tint.$description: Pale-blue informational surface (tint chips, callouts). Pair with `tint-foreground`.

tint-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `tint` surface (blue.700 on blue.100, 6.49:1 AA).

info.$description: Informational accent — the border + leading icon of an info callout/alert, painted on the pale `tint` surface. The source's `accent` role: brand blue.500 light → blue.300 dark (the theme-aware brand accent, distinct from `primary` which stays blue.500 in dark).

chip-border.$description: Resting border of an unselected filter chip — the pale brand-blue outline (blue.200 light → blue.300 @ 45% dark) that reads as a quiet, tappable outline before selection. Source `chipBorder`.

header.$description: Brand chrome band (top nav / app header) — the brand-forward blue.500. Pair with `header-foreground`; header text is large/bold brand or logo (white on blue.500 is 3.69:1, the large/bold ≥3:1 carve-out, not normal-weight body — see AA note in ADR-0013 §7).

header-foreground.$description: Text, logo and icon colour on the `header` band (white).

success.$description: Positive-status fill, border or icon. Pair with `success-foreground` (white is large/bold on green.500 = 3.68:1).

success-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `success` fill.

success-tint.$description: Pale-green success surface behind a success callout/alert — the source's success tint. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the `success` border + ✓ icon carry the semantic. Theme-aware: pale-green light → deep-green dark.

warning.$description: Caution-status fill, border or icon (functional amber). Pair with `warning-foreground` — white FAILS AA on amber, so the foreground is dark ink.

warning-foreground.$description: Dark ink on a `warning` amber fill (neutral.950, 5.14:1 on amber.500 / 8.99:1 on amber.400 dark).

warning-tint.$description: Pale-amber caution surface behind a warning callout/alert — the source's warn tint. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the `warning` border + ⚠ icon carry the semantic. Theme-aware: pale-amber light → deep-amber dark.

destructive.$description: Error/danger/live fill, border or icon — destructive actions, invalid state, live indicators. Pair with `destructive-foreground`.

destructive-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `destructive` fill.

destructive-tint.$description: Pale-red field/error surface (danger tint) behind an invalid input — the source's `dangerTint`. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the destructive border + `⚠` message carry the error. Theme-aware: pale-red light → deep-maroon dark.

live.$description: Flat live/danger red (#C81E1E) — the fill of a `live` badge ("в эфире") and the border + leading icon of a danger callout/alert. The source's `danger`/`live` constant: INVARIANT across themes (same #C81E1E on the light page and the dark maroon tint), so it does NOT track the theme-flipping `destructive` token (whose dark variant lifts to red.400 for AA on the dark card fill). Pair with `live-foreground`.

live-foreground.$description: Text, icon and pulse-dot colour on a `live` fill — white in both themes (white on #C81E1E = 5.74:1 AA).

border.$description: Structural border — the hard near-black outline (neutral.900) of the neo-brutalist frame (2px). Not the subtle divider; that is `hairline`.

hairline.$description: Subtle 1px divider/rule between rows and list items, where a hard structural `border` would be too heavy.

input.$description: Border colour of form inputs and controls — the same hard structural outline as `border`.

ring.$description: The keyboard-focus ring colour (blue.300); the `focus` shadow (`shadow.focus`) is blue.300 @ 50%.

elevation.$description: Primary cast-shadow tone for the hard offset shadows (`shadow.md`/`lg`). Theme-aware: blue.700 light → blue.300 dark.

elevation-soft.$description: Softer cast-shadow tone for the small offset shadows (`shadow.sm`/`base`). Theme-aware: blue.200 light → blue.600 dark.

space.$description: §09 «Раскладка и ритм» semantic spacing ROLES over the §03 4px scale — space is composed by ROLE, not by eye. Each role names WHERE a gap belongs so surfaces stay in rhythm; the source states a small set per role and these tokens carry the canonical (modal) value, with the fuller range noted in the package README. Surfaced as Tailwind spacing utilities via the `--spacing-<role>` @theme namespace: `p-inset`, `gap-controls`, `gap-inline`, `space-y-section`, `px-gutter`, `-mx-gutter` (bleed), … The two responsive roles compose with the `layout:` breakpoint variant: STACK collapses to 0 on mobile (`space-y-0 layout:space-y-stack`); GUTTER is a fixed 16px on mobile and the clamp on desktop (owned by the Container primitive). DAY-BAND is 0 — the day plate bleeds flush to its cards.

gutter.$description: Container horizontal gutter on desktop (≥ layout breakpoint): clamp(16px, 4vw, 48px). On mobile the Container uses a fixed 16px gutter instead (edge-to-edge, so day-band/cards bleed cleanly by -16px). Also the negative-margin bleed unit (`-mx-gutter`).

inset.$description: Padding INSIDE cards, panels and buttons (canonical 24px; the source spans 16 · 20 · 24 · 30 — reach for `p-4`/`p-5` for tighter insets).

stack.$description: Gap BETWEEN cards in a list — 28px on desktop, collapsing to 0 on mobile where cards go edge-to-edge. Compose responsively: `space-y-0 layout:space-y-stack`.

section.$description: Rhythm BETWEEN meaning blocks / sections (canonical 48px; the source spans 44 · 48).

controls.$description: Gap between adjacent controls — chips, buttons, fields (canonical 12px; the source spans 8 · 10 · 12 — `gap-2`/`gap-2.5` for tighter clusters).

inline.$description: Gap on the inline axis — icon ↔ text, label ↔ value (canonical 8px; the source spans 6 · 8 — `gap-1.5` for the tighter pair).

day-band.$description: The day plate sits FLUSH (0) against its cards and bleeds to the container edges — see the `-mx-gutter` bleed pattern.

micro-label.$description: Neo-brutalist micro-label / eyebrow: 10px extrabold, wide +.12em tracking, UPPERCASE. Section eyebrows, chip labels, meta tags.
